title: Crepidium radicicola
taxonomy: Crepidium radicicola belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a genus of orchids that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The species is classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land rather than in water or on trees. The classification of Crepidium radicicola is further divided into its kingdom, which is Plantae, and its phylum, which is Magnoliophyta. This species is also known as Epidendrum radicicola, but the name has been changed to Crepidium radicicola in recent years.
uses: Crepidium radicicola is reported to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is said to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating infections.
